title: Brunelleschi, Dome (quiz)
topic_path: Sculpture and architecture in central Italy

assessment_items.question_content:
**When the Duomo was built, it was the highest dome that had ever been raised, until the rebuilding of [[☃ dropdown 1]].**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2aa2ecccc4a0cf0be777347c102002ff.jpg)
hints:
**When it was built, it was the highest dome that had ever been raised, until the rebuilding of St. Peter's Basilica in Rome.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/brunelleschi--dome-of-the-cathedral-of-florence--1420-36)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Duomo's dome was almost as wide as the[[☃ dropdown 1]] dome.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2aa2ecccc4a0cf0be777347c102002ff.jpg)
hints:
**The Duomo's dome was almost as wide as the Pantheon's dome.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/brunelleschi--dome-of-the-cathedral-of-florence--1420-36)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Brunelleschi came up with a way of constructing a dome that did not require the use of [[☃ dropdown 1]].**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2aa2ecccc4a0cf0be777347c102002ff.jpg)
hints:
**Brunelleschi came up with a way of constructing a dome that did not require the use of wooden scaffolding.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/brunelleschi--dome-of-the-cathedral-of-florence--1420-36)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Brunelleschi's dome?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2aa2ecccc4a0cf0be777347c102002ff.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Interior chains hold the dome in and counter the downward and outward thrust of the dome.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/brunelleschi--dome-of-the-cathedral-of-florence--1420-36)**

choices.content: To help manage the weight of the dome, Brunelleschi created a hollow dome with inner and outer shells.
Brunelleschi created ribs that were supported horizontally that helped to bear the weight of the dome.
The bricks that make up the dome were placed in locking pattern that helped to secure them in place.
The bricks are hollow and therefore counter the downward and outward thrust of the dome.
